name: Concorde Bank
description_short: Community bank operating in Minnesota since 1910, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and lending services across Blomkest and Willmar locations.
description_long:
Concorde Bank is a community-focused financial institution with over 105 years of banking experience, established in 1910. The bank operates two physical locations in Blomkest and Willmar, Minnesota, and emphasizes personalized service combined with modern digital banking capabilities. The institution positions itself as committed to maintaining sound financial practices with high integrity and professional staff responsive to community needs.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, personal loans, and debit cards. On the business side, Concorde provides commercial checking and savings accounts, commercial and agricultural loans, and merchant card services. Their mortgage division handles home purchase financing and refinancing with multiple loan program options. Additional services include online banking, mobile banking, e-statements, Visa gift cards, ATM access, and fee schedules with calculators for financial planning.

Concorde Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community relationships and personalized service in a digital age. The bank maintains named loan officers with NMLS certifications (Scott Wahl, Earl Huber, Larry Knutson, and Mitch Baker) available for business and consumer lending consultation. They explicitly market competitive CD rates and offer flexibility in how customers choose to bank—whether through in-person visits, online banking, or mobile applications. The institution updated operating hours as of January 1, 2025, indicating ongoing operational responsiveness.

As a small community bank with only two branch locations, Concorde Bank's primary limitation is geographic reach—customers outside the Blomkest and Willmar areas would need to rely entirely on digital banking services. While the website emphasizes service quality and product variety, specific information about interest rates, loan terms, fee structures, and minimum account balances is not publicly displayed on their website, requiring direct contact with branch staff. The bank's size compared to larger regional or national institutions may affect product innovation speed and competitive pricing on certain products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal loans (home, auto, RV, and other purposes)
Mortgage loans and home financing programs
Online banking and mobile banking platforms
pros: Community bank with 105+ years of established history and stability since 1910
Two convenient physical branch locations in Blomkest and Willmar with personalized loan officer consultants
NMLS-certified lending officers available for business and consumer lending consultations
Full suite of services including personal banking, business banking, mortgages, and agricultural loans
Modern digital banking options (online banking, mobile banking, e-statements) combined with in-person service
Competitive CD rates available and marketed as a lending advantage
Agricultural lending specifically mentioned, indicating specialization in rural Minnesota communities
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only two branch locations in small Minnesota towns, restricting in-person access for most customers
Website lacks transparency on specific interest rates, loan terms, APRs, and account fee structures
No information provided about FDIC insurance coverage limits or regulatory standing
Smaller institution compared to regional/national banks may offer fewer product variations and less competitive rates
Website does not display current account minimum balances, application timelines, or loan approval criteria
best_for: Rural Minnesota residents and business owners in Blomkest and Willmar seeking personalized community banking relationships
Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized commercial and agricultural lending services
Small business owners wanting direct access to named loan officers for customized lending solutions
Customers preferring established, local institutions with long community histories over larger corporate banks
diagnosis: Concorde Bank is best suited for individuals and small business owners in rural Minnesota (Blomkest and Willmar areas) who value personal relationships with lenders and community banking values. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside these two towns will find themselves entirely dependent on digital banking, and the lack of publicly available pricing information requires direct contact with branches before committing to any product.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
